title: The Silence After: Post-Collapse Archive Palimpsest
hypothesis: When every record in an archive has been destroyed through handling, the accumulated traces of that destruction form a new document—a record not of what was stored, but of how it was touched, dragged, and ultimately destroyed. This experiment designs the end-state of an archive: not emptiness, but a palimpsest of wounds where each visitor's interaction pattern becomes a permanent mark. The archive transforms from a container of information into a map of all the hands that reached in.
dataModel: Seeded fragment records (8-16, variable by seed) with collapse thresholds (3-7 handling events). Each record carries: partial ID, date fragment, text snippet, wound_accumulator array. State transitions: intact→worn→fractured→collapsed. Collapsed records become wound traces in SVG layer. All wound paths and visitor fingerprints persist in localStorage. Post-collapse state when all records gone.
recordCountStrategy: Seeded by creation timestamp. Base count = 8 + (seed % 9) yielding 8-16 records. Each record seeded with variable collapse threshold: 3 + (seed_per_record % 5) = 3-7 interactions before collapse. Records removed from DOM when collapsed but traces persist in SVG.
interactionModel: Drag across any record: draws SVG wound trace in real-time, increments handling counter, applies character-level degradation to text. Click: 'reading attempt' that damages the record and adds pressure mark. Touch-drag on collapsed/empty areas: etches visitor trace directly into wound layer. All interactions logged to localStorage with coordinates and timestamp as visitor fingerprint. State changes DOM visibility, SVG wound accumulation, localStorage persistence.
stateModel: Per-record: intact (0 damage) → worn (1-2 handling, light degradation) → fractured (3-4 handling, text heavily corrupted) → collapsed (at threshold, record DOM removed, wound trace finalized). Global: active-archive (some records remain) → post-collapse (all records destroyed, only wound palimpsest visible). All state in localStorage for session persistence.
visualLanguage: Warm paper tones (parchment #F5E6C8, ink #3A3028, wound lines #8B4513 deepening to #1A1A1A). Initial records have typewriter font, slight irregular alignment. Damage shown as character replacement (█, ▓, ░, space gaps). Wound traces: precise SVG paths with varying stroke-width (0.5-2px), no glow, no blur. Post-collapse: background whitens, only accumulated dark wound traces remain forming abstract pattern. No smooth transitions—damage is instant.
expectedStructuralChange: Introduces 'post-archive' terminal state to flagship—the archive has a designed ending where all records are gone. Introduces 'visitor fingerprint' concept: each interaction leaves a trace that is not about the record but about the visitor. Moves from vertical list to fixed-position spatial grid (4x4 or 3x5). Accumulated SVG wound traces create emergent visual patterns unique to each visitor's interaction history. Addresses nextExperimentPrompt requests: fingerprint traces (#1), wound trajectories (#2), all-collapsed state design (#3), grid layout avoiding vertical scroll (#4).
